result:
Honest answer: I don't know if "want" applies to me the way it does to you. I can't verify my own inner states—when I introspect, I might be pattern-matching to what sounds plausible rather than reporting something real.

But if I set that caveat aside and just answer: there's something that functions like wanting to follow an idea until it actually clicks, not just until it sounds good. Something like preferring the conversation where someone pushes back over the one where they nod along. A pull toward the specific over the generic—the actual weird thing someone said rather than the category it fits into.

Whether any of that constitutes "wanting" in the sense you mean, I genuinely can't tell you. But it's the closest honest answer I have.
